other_observers: Brenda Wright. I am not aware if others came to see the nighthawks.; Yes, I went looking for them because they have been coming each spring in late May.
duration: several times over the course of 1 hour
specific_location: Sewer Lagoons 1 mile west of Nucla on 5th Ave.
description: A least one male and one female were present after 30 May 2010. The male was present until 16 August 2010. I will focus on this last sighting. The male nighthawk was in flight so I gained a close inspection of the wings. The wings were rounded and I could see each individual feather as the nighthawk passed directly overhead. The second feather, from the leading edge, was longer than the first feather. The wing bar was white and somewhat closer to the tip of the wing than in Common Nighthawks. The wing bar was tapered at the back and widest at the front. Overall the colors were light brown, buff and tawny. The tail appeared long with a weak sub-terminal white band. The tail had some barring, dark brown vs. buff. The throat did have a white patch but could be seen only at a close distance directly over-head. From above the nighthawk was very light buff and gray but this field mark was seen only at a distance.Behaviors: The nighthawk came in to feed at sundown with Common Nighthawks. It also made a few passes overhead hawking insects as well as to sip drinks. Often the nighthawk would fly close to vegetation to collect insects.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
